message.content:
Find the v1 Carousel component in packages/react/src/ (NOT v0). Read its main component file, types, and any stories. I need to understand:
1. The full props API
2. Whether it enforces a fixed height (sliderHeight prop or similar)
3. How slides are passed (children? items prop?)
4. Whether it exposes navigation controls or handles them internally
5. Any constraints that would make it unsuitable for variable-height product cards

Return the file paths and relevant code snippets.

content.text: I'll help you explore the v1 Carousel component. Let me start by searching for it in the packages/react structure.

input.description: Search for Carousel files in v1 react package

content.text: Good! I can see there's a v1 Carousel at `/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/`. Let me also check for stories and the index file.
Let me search more broadly for Carousel stories:
Interesting - I see CardCarousel and ReviewCarousel but let me check if there's a v1 Carousel stories file:
Let me check what's actually in the v1 Carousel component itself:

Slides outside the visible window are replaced 39→ * with a placeholder until scrolled into view. 40→ * @default false 41→ */ 42→ lazy?: boolean; 43→ 44→ /** 45→ * Number of additional offscreen slides to pre-load. 46→ * Only effective when `lazy=true`. 47→ * @default 1 48→ */ 49→ lazyOffset?: number; 50→ 51→ /** 52→ * Allow slide navigation to wrap from last slide to first (and vice versa). 53→ * @default true 54→ */ 55→ loop?: boolean; 56→ 57→ /** 58→ * Position of the thumbnail navigator when `thumbnails` are provided. 59→ * @default 'left' 60→ */ 61→ navigatorSide?: 'left' | 'bottom'; 62→ 63→ /** 64→ * Background color of the slider area. 65→ * @default 'white' 66→ */ 67→ sliderBg?: 'white' | 'gray' | 'transparent'; 68→ 69→ /** 70→ * Height of the slider area. 71→ * @default '350px' 72→ */ 73→ sliderHeight?: string; 74→ 75→ /** 76→ * Inline style applied to the slider area `
` (excludes the thumbnail navigator). 77→ */ 78→ sliderStyle?: React.CSSProperties; 79→ 80→ /** 81→ * Controls the origin of the slides within the slider viewport. 82→ * When `undefined`, the origin is auto-calculated: `'auto'` when `slidesPerView > 1`, `'center'` otherwise. 83→ * @default undefined 84→ */ 85→ sliderOrigin?: 'auto' | 'center'; 86→ 87→ /** 88→ * Array of slide content elements (usually images). Required. 89→ */ 90→ slides: React.ReactElement[]; 91→ 92→ /** 93→ * Number of slides visible simultaneously. 94→ * @default 1 95→ */ 96→ slidesPerView?: number; 97→ 98→ /** 99→ * Spacing in pixels between adjacent slides. 100→ * @default 0 101→ */ 102→ slidesSpacing?: number; 103→ 104→ /** 105→ * Array of thumbnail image elements for the thumbnail navigator. 106→ * When provided, dot navigation is hidden. 107→ */ 108→ thumbnails?: React.ReactElement[]; 109→ 110→ /** 111→ * Size of thumbnail items. 112→ * @default 'medium' 113→ */ 114→ thumbSize?: 'small' | 'medium'; 115→ 116→ /** 117→ * Called when the keen-slider instance is created. 118→ * Provides access to the KeenSliderInstance for programmatic control. 119→ */ 120→ onCreated?: (slider: KeenSliderInstance) => void; 121→} & React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<'div'>; 122→ 123→/** 124→ * Carousel component for displaying ordered sequences of slides with 125→ * thumbnail navigation, dot navigation, and prev/next arrow controls. 126→ * Built on keen-slider for touch/drag sliding and loop support. 127→ */ 128→export const Carousel = React.forwardRef( 129→ ( 130→ { 131→ arrows = true, 132→ arrowPosition = 'inside', 133→ arrowOffset = 40, 134→ dots = true, 135→ lazy = false, 136→ lazyOffset = 1, 137→ loop = true, 138→ navigatorSide = 'left', 139→ sliderBg = 'white', 140→ sliderHeight = '350px', 141→ sliderStyle, 142→ sliderOrigin, 143→ slides, 144→ slidesPerView = 1, 145→ slidesSpacing = 0, 146→ thumbnails, 147→ thumbSize = 'medium', 148→ onCreated, 149→ className, 150→ ...restProps 151→ }, 152→ ref, 153→ ) => { 154→ const [loaded, setLoaded] = useState([]); 155→ const [currentSlide, setCurrentSlide] = useState(0); 156→ 157→ const origin = sliderOrigin ?? (slidesPerView > 1 ? 'auto' : 'center'); 158→ 159→ const [keenSliderCallbackRef, instanceRef] = useKeenSlider({ 160→ initial: 0, 161→ loop, 162→ slides: { 163→ origin, 164→ perView: slidesPerView, 165→ spacing: slidesSpacing, 166→ }, 167→ animationEnded(slider) { 168→ setCurrentSlide(slider.track.details.rel); 169→ }, 170→ slideChanged(slider) { 171→ setCurrentSlide(slider.track.details.rel); 172→ }, 173→ created: onCreated, 174→ }); 175→ 176→ /* 177→ * Resize keen-slider when the bounding div is resized. 178→ * keen-slider automatically listens for window resize, but needs manual 179→ * updates when layout changes independently of the window. 180→ * boundingBoxRef is a RefObject (has .current) required by useResizeObserver. 181→ * keenSliderCallbackRef is a callback ref (function only, no .current). 182→ */ 183→ const boundingBoxRef = useRef(null); 184→ useResizeObserver(boundingBoxRef, () => instanceRef.current?.update()); 185→ 186→ useEffect(() => { 187→ const newLoaded = [...loaded]; 188→ newLoaded[currentSlide] = true; 189→ for (let i = 1; i < slidesPerView + lazyOffset; i++) { 190→ newLoaded[(currentSlide - i + slides.length) % slides.length] = true; 191→ newLoaded[(currentSlide + i + slides.length) % slides.length] = true; 192→ } 193→ setLoaded(newLoaded); 194→ // This dependency array is intentionally non-exhaustive — matches v0 behavior. 195→ // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 196→ }, [currentSlide]); 197→ 198→ const hasThumbs = !!thumbnails?.length; 199→ const showArrows = slides.length >= 2 && arrows; 200→ const cssArrowOffset = typeof arrowOffset === 'number' ? 1→export { Carousel, type CarouselProps } from './Carousel';
     2→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→# ComponentSpec: Carousel
     2→
     3→## Summary
     4→
     5→Carousel is a content slider component for displaying ordered sequences of slides (typically images) with thumbnail navigation, dot navigation, and prev/next arrow controls. It is built on [keen-slider](https://keen-slider.io/docs) — an existing package dependency — which provides touch/drag sliding, loop support, and multi-slide layouts. Base UI does not have a carousel/slider for content navigation (only a range-input `Slider`), so Base UI is not applicable. The correct approach is plain HTML/CSS + keen-slider.
     6→
     7→The v1 migration removes all MUI dependencies: `MuiIconButton` → `<button>`, `Skeleton` → CSS placeholder, `styled()` → CSS Modules, `Box` (v0 layout) → `<div>`.
     8→
     9→## API
    10→
    11→### Props
    12→
    13→```typescript
    14→type CarouselProps = {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Show prev/next navigation arrows.
    17→   * Arrows are automatically hidden when there is only 1 slide.
    18→   * @default true
    19→   */
    20→  arrows?: boolean;
    21→
    22→  /**
    23→   * Position of arrows relative to the slider area.
    24→   * @default 'inside'
    25→   */
    26→  arrowPosition?: 'inside' | 'outside';
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Horizontal offset of arrows from the slider edge.
    30→   * Only effective when `arrowPosition='outside'`.
    31→   * Accepts a number (interpreted as pixels) or a CSS string value.
    32→   * @default 40
    33→   */
    34→  arrowOffset?: string | number;
    35→
    36→  /**
    37→   * Show dot navigation below the slider.
    38→   * Dots are hidden when `thumbnails` are provided.
    39→   * @default true
    40→   */
    41→  dots?: boolean;
    42→
    43→  /**
    44→   * Enable lazy loading. Slides outside the visible window are replaced
    45→   * with a placeholder until scrolled into view.
    46→   * "Visible window" = slidesPerView + lazyOffset slides around currentSlide.
    47→   * @default false
    48→   */
    49→  lazy?: boolean;
    50→
    51→  /**
    52→   * Number of additional offscreen slides to pre-load.
    53→   * Only effective when `lazy=true`.
    54→   * @default 1
    55→   */
    56→  lazyOffset?: number;
    57→
    58→  /**
    59→   * Allow slide navigation to wrap from last slide to first (and vice versa).
    60→   * @default true
    61→   */
    62→  loop?: boolean;
    63→
    64→  /**
    65→   * Position of the thumbnail navigator when `thumbnails` are provided.
    66→   * @default 'left'
    67→   */
    68→  navigatorSide?: 'left' | 'bottom';
    69→
    70→  /**
    71→   * Background color of the slider area.
    72→   * @default 'white'
    73→   */
    74→  sliderBg?: 'white' | 'gray' | 'transparent';
    75→
    76→  /**
    77→   * Height of the slider area.
    78→   * @default '350px'
    79→   */
    80→  sliderHeight?: string;
    81→
    82→  /**
    83→   * Inline style applied to the slider area `<div>` (excludes the thumbnail navigator).
    84→   */
    85→  sliderStyle?: React.CSSProperties;
    86→
    87→  /**
    88→   * Controls the origin of the slides within the slider viewport.
    89→   * When `undefined`, the origin is auto-calculated: `'auto'` when `slidesPerView > 1`, `'center'` otherwise.
    90→   * @default undefined
    91→   */
    92→  sliderOrigin?: 'auto' | 'center';
    93→
    94→  /**
    95→   * Array of slide content elements (usually images). Required.
    96→   */
    97→  slides: React.ReactElement[];
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Number of slides visible simultaneously.
   101→   * @default 1
   102→   */
   103→  slidesPerView?: number;
   104→
   105→  /**
   106→   * Spacing in pixels between adjacent slides.
   107→   * @default 0
   108→   */
   109→  slidesSpacing?: number;
   110→
   111→  /**
   112→   * Array of thumbnail image elements for the thumbnail navigator.
   113→   * When provided, dot navigation is hidden.
   114→   */
   115→  thumbnails?: React.ReactElement[];
   116→
   117→  /**
   118→   * Size of thumbnail items.
   119→   * @default 'medium'
   120→   */
   121→  thumbSize?: 'small' | 'medium';
   122→
   123→  /**
   124→   * Called when the keen-slider instance is created.
   125→   * Provides access to the KeenSliderInstance for programmatic control
   126→   * (e.g., `slider.moveToIdx(n)`).
   127→   */
   128→  onCreated?: (slider: KeenSliderInstance) => void;
   129→} & React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<'div'>;
   130→```
   131→
   132→`KeenSliderInstance` is imported from `keen-slider` (existing dependency). It is used only in the `onCreated` callback type.
   133→
   134→### Variants
   135→
   136→No single `variant` prop. Visual variations are controlled by individual props:
   137→
   138→| Prop | Values | Visual Effect |
   139→|------|--------|---------------|
   140→| `sliderBg` | `'white'` | Slider area: white background |
   141→| `sliderBg` | `'gray'` | Slider area: light gray background |
   142→| `sliderBg` | `'transparent'` | Slider area: transparent background |
   143→| `arrowPosition` | `'inside'` | Arrows float inside the slider with 8px side margins |
   144→| `arrowPosition` | `'outside'` | Arrows positioned outside the slider edges, offset by `arrowOffset` |
   145→| `navigatorSide` | `'left'` | Thumbnail strip runs vertically to the left of the slider |
   146→| `navigatorSide` | `'bottom'` | Thumbnail strip runs horizontally below the slider |
   147→| `thumbSize` | `'medium'` | 56×56px thumbnails |
   148→| `thumbSize` | `'small'` | 48×48px thumbnails |
   149→
   150→### HTML Attributes
   151→
   152→Extends `React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<'div'>`. All standard div attributes (`className`, `style`, `data-*`, `aria-*`, `id`, etc.) pass through to the root container `<div>`.
   153→
   154→### Ref Target
   155→
   156→Ref forwards to the root container `<div>` (the outermost element wrapping both slider and thumbnail navigator). This is non-obvious because the component has multiple meaningful sub-elements, but the root is the correct target for layout/positioning purposes.
   157→
   158→## Visual Spec
   159→
   160→### Structure
   161→
   162→```
   163→Root container <div>                             ← flex, full-width
   164→├── Slider area <div.slider>                     ← relative, sliderHeight tall
   165→│   ├── Arrow container <div>                    ← absolute overlay, full bounds
   166→│   │   ├── Left arrow <button>
   167→│   │   └── Right arrow <button>
   168→│   ├── keen-slider <div>                        ← full height
   169→│   │   └── Slide items <div> × N               ← flex, centered content
   170→│   └── Dots container <div>                     ← absolute, bottom
   171→│       └── Dot <button> × N
   172→└── Thumbnail navigator <div>                    ← flex, centered
   173→    └── Thumbnails strip <div>                   ← flex row or column
   174→        └── Thumbnail item <button> × N          ← see Accessibility section
   175→```
   176→
   177→### Tokens
   178→
   179→#### Layout
   180→
   181→| Property | Value | Token |
   182→|----------|-------|-------|
   183→| Root: display | flex | — |
   184→| Root: width | 100% | — |
   185→| Root: height | max-content | — |
   186→| Root: flex-direction | row-reverse | when `navigatorSide='left'` |
   187→| Root: flex-direction | column | when `navigatorSide='bottom'` |
   188→| Slider area: height | `sliderHeight` prop (default `350px`) | — |
   189→| Slider area: width (no thumbs, or `navigatorSide='bottom'`) | 100% | — |
   190→| Slider area: width (left thumbs, `thumbSize='medium'`) | `calc(100% - 72px)` | thumb(56px) + gap(16px) |
   191→| Slider area: width (left thumbs, `thumbSize='small'`) | `calc(100% - 64px)` | thumb(48px) + gap(16px) |
   192→| Slider area: overflow | visible | (arrows may overflow) |
   193→| keen-slider div height | 100% | — |
   194→| Slide item: display | flex | — |
   195→| Slide item: align-items | center | — |
   196→| Slide item: justify-content | center | — |
   197→| Slide item: cursor | grab | — |
   198→
   199→#### Arrow Button
   200→
   201→| Property | Value | Notes |
   202→|----------|-------|-------|
   203→| Size | 32×32px | `--size-tappable-stingy` |
   204→| Border-radius | 50% | `--radius-round` |
   205→| Background (default) | `#ffffff` | `--neutral-background-primary` |
   206→| Background (hover) | `#f3f3f3` | Unspecified — v0 value; closest v3: `--primitives-color-neutral-gray100` (#f5f5f5) |
   207→| Background (active/pressed) | `rgba(24, 24, 24, 0.1)` | Unspecified — v0 value; closest v3: `--interactive-background-weak` |
   208→| Background (disabled) | transparent | — |
   209→| Box shadow (enabled states) | `0px 4px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08)` | Raw value — no token |
   210→| Box shadow (disabled) | none | — |
   211→| Icon color (enabled) | `#4a4a4a` | `--primitives-color-neutral-gray700` — no semantic match; needs Figma verification |
   212→| Icon color (disabled) | `#c6c6c6` | Unspecified — v0 value; closest v3: `--interactive-icon-disabled` (rgba(0,0,0,0.18)=#d1d1d1, approximate) |
   213→| Cursor (disabled) | default | — |
   214→| Focus-visible | outline `2px solid --interactive-border-focus`, offset `2px` | Not in v0; added in v1 |
   215→| Margin (arrowPosition=inside) | `0 8px` horizontal | — |
   216→| Position (arrowPosition=outside) | absolute, left arrow at `left: -arrowOffset`, right at `right: -arrowOffset` | — |
   217→| z-index | 1 | above slide content |
   218→| Left arrow icon | `ArrowNextIcon` transformed `scale(-1, 1)` | — |
   219→| Right arrow icon | `ArrowNextIcon` | — |
   220→| Arrow icon size | 24px (icon font-size inherited from button) | — |
   221→
   222→Arrow container (wrapper div): `position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; pointer-events: none`. Arrow buttons themselves: `pointer-events: auto`.
   223→
   224→#### Dots
   225→
   226→| Property | Value | Notes |
   227→|----------|-------|-------|
   228→| Dot element | `<button>` | (v0 used `<div>`) |
   229→| Dot size (::after pseudo-element) | 8×8px | — |
   230→| Dot padding | 4px all sides | (creates larger click target) |
   231→| Dot ::after border-radius | 61px | — |
   232→| Dot ::after color (inactive) | `#ffffff` | opacity: 0.6 |
   233→| Dot ::after color (active) | `#4a4a4a` | Unspecified — v0 value; needs Figma verification |
   234→| Dot ::after opacity (inactive) | 0.6 | — |
   235→| Dot ::after opacity (active/hover) | 1.0 | — |
   236→| Dots container: position | absolute, bottom: 0 | — |
   237→| Dots container: full width | left: 0; right: 0 | — |
   238→| Dots container: padding | 20px 0 | — |
   239→| Dots container: display | flex; justify-content: center | — |
   240→| Inactive dot cursor | pointer | — |
   241→| Active dot cursor | default | — |
   242→
   243→#### Thumbnails
   244→
   245→| Property | Value | Notes |
   246→|----------|-------|-------|
   247→| Thumbnail element | `<button>` | (v0 used `<div>`; see Accessibility) |
   248→| Thumbnail size (medium) | 56×56px | — |
   249→| Thumbnail size (small) | 48×48px | — |
   250→| Thumbnail border-radius | 4px | `--radius-sm` |
   251→| Thumbnail background | `#ffffff` | `--neutral-background-primary` |
   252→| Thumbnail border (default) | 1px solid `#f4f4f4` | Unspecified — v0 value; closest v3: `--neutral-border-weak` (approximate) |
   253→| Thumbnail border (hover) | 1px solid `#c6c6c6` | Unspecified — v0 value; needs Figma verification |
   254→| Thumbnail border (selected) | 1px solid `#181818` | `--primitives-color-neutral-gray990` — no semantic match; needs Figma verification |
   255→| Thumbnail border transition | `border 0.1s ease-out` | from v0 |
   256→| Thumbnail cursor | pointer (unselected) / default (selected) | — |
   257→| Thumbnail overflow | hidden | — |
   258→| Thumbnail flex-shrink | 0 | — |
   259→| Thumbnail box-sizing | border-box | — |
   260→| Thumbnail inset shadow | `inset 0px 0px 8px 4px #f4f4f4` | Decorative — raw value |
   261→| Thumbnail mix-blend-mode | darken | Tints non-white backgrounds |
   262→| Thumbnail ::before overlay (border effect) | position absolute, full bounds, border-radius 4px | trick to show border inside image |
   263→| Thumbnails gap | 8px | `--space-detailLayout-xl` |
   264→| Thumbnails (left): flex-direction | column | — |
   265→| Thumbnails (left): margin-right | 16px | `--space-componentLayout-md` |
   266→| Thumbnails (left): overflow-y | auto | — |
   267→| Thumbnails (left): max-height | `sliderHeight` value | — |
   268→| Thumbnails (bottom): margin-top | 16px | `--space-componentLayout-md` |
   269→| Thumbnails (bottom): justify-content | flex-start (left-aligned) | — |
   270→| Thumbnails (bottom): overflow-x | auto | — |
   271→
   272→#### Slider Background
   273→
   274→| `sliderBg` | Color | Token |
   275→|------------|-------|-------|
   276→| `'white'` | `#ffffff` | `--neutral-background-primary` |
   277→| `'gray'` | `#f4f4f4` | Unspecified — v0 value; closest v3: `--neutral-background-subtle` (verify) |
   278→| `'transparent'` | `transparent` | — |
   279→
   280→#### Lazy Loading Placeholder
   281→
   282→| Property | Value |
   283→|----------|-------|
   284→| Element | `<div>` with full width/height |
   285→| Background | `--neutral-background-subtle` |
   286→| No skeleton animation | — |
   287→
   288→v0 used MUI `<Skeleton variant="rectangular">`. v1 has no Skeleton component. A plain CSS div is sufficient.
   289→
   290→### States
   291→
   292→#### Arrow Button
   293→
   294→| State | Background | Shadow | Other |
   295→|-------|------------|--------|-------|
   296→| Default | `#ffffff` | `0px 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` | — |
   297→| Hover | `#f3f3f3` | `0px 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` | — |
   298→| Active | `rgba(24,24,24,0.1)` | `0px 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` | — |
   299→| Disabled | transparent | none | `disabled` attribute, cursor default |
   300→| Focus-visible | — | — | 2px focus outline |
   301→
   302→#### Thumbnail
   303→
   304→| State | Border |
   305→|-------|--------|
   306→| Default | `1px solid #f4f4f4` |
   307→| Hover | `1px solid #c6c6c6` |
   308→| Selected | `1px solid #181818`, cursor default |
   309→
   310→### Transitions
   311→
   312→- Thumbnail border: `transition: border 0.1s ease-out` (from v0)
   313→- Arrow hover/active background: none
   314→- Dot color transition: none
   315→- Slide animation: handled internally by keen-slider
   316→
   317→## Behavior
   318→
   319→### Sliding
   320→
   321→- keen-slider is initialized with `{ initial: 0, loop, slides: { origin, perView: slidesPerView, spacing: slidesSpacing } }`
   322→- Slide `origin`: uses `sliderOrigin` prop if provided; otherwise auto-calculated as `'auto'` when `slidesPerView > 1`, `'center'` otherwise
   323→- Current slide index is tracked in local state; updated on `slideChanged` and `animationEnded` keen-slider events
   324→
   325→### Arrows
   326→
   327→- Left arrow: calls `prev()` on keen-slider instance
   328→- Right arrow: calls `next()` on keen-slider instance
   329→- Left arrow disabled condition: `!loop && currentSlide === 0`
   330→- Right arrow disabled condition: `!loop && currentSlide === slides.length - 1`
   331→- Arrows hidden: when `arrows=false` OR `slides.length < 2`
   332→- Arrow click stops propagation before calling prev/next
   333→
   334→### Dots
   335→
   336→- Shown only when: `!thumbnails?.length && dots && slides.length > 1`
   337→- Clicking dot N calls `instanceRef.current?.moveToIdx(N)`
   338→- Active dot = currentSlide index
   339→
   340→### Thumbnail Navigator
   341→
   342→- Shown only when `thumbnails?.length > 0` AND the keen-slider instance is initialized
   343→- Clicking thumbnail N calls `instanceRef.current?.moveToIdx(N)`
   344→- Selected thumbnail = currentSlide index
   345→
   346→### Resize
   347→
   348→- A `ResizeObserver` watches the slider bounding box div
   349→- On resize, calls `instanceRef.current?.update()` to re-calculate keen-slider layout
   350→- This handles cases where the carousel is inside a layout that changes size independently of the window
   351→
   352→### Lazy Loading
   353→
   354→- Per-slide `loaded[]` boolean array tracks which slides have been rendered
   355→- On `currentSlide` change: marks `currentSlide`, and `±i` neighbors for `i` in `[1, slidesPerView + lazyOffset)` as loaded
   356→- Unloaded slides show the CSS placeholder instead of slide content
   357→
   358→### `onCreated` Callback
   359→
   360→- Called from keen-slider's `created` hook
   361→- Provides the `KeenSliderInstance` object for programmatic slider control by the consumer
   362→
   363→## Accessibility
   364→
   365→### Current v0 Status (for comparison)
   366→
   367→- Arrow buttons: `<MuiIconButton>` — focusable, no aria-label
   368→- Dots: `<div onClick>` — not keyboard accessible
   369→- Thumbnails: `<div onClick>` — not keyboard accessible
   370→
   371→### v1 Requirements
   372→
   373→- Arrow buttons: `<button>` with `aria-label="Previous slide"` and `aria-label="Next slide"`. Disabled via native `disabled` attribute.
   374→- Dots: `<button>` elements with `aria-label="Go to slide {n}"` (1-based). Active dot: `aria-current="true"` or `aria-pressed="true"`.
   375→- Thumbnails: `<button>` elements. Selected thumbnail: `aria-pressed="true"` or similar.
   376→- keen-slider container: no explicit role needed (content is navigable via arrows/dots).
   377→- All icons: `aria-hidden="true"`.
   378→
   379→Upgrading dots and thumbnails from `<div>` to `<button>` is an accessibility improvement (keyboard navigation). It is not an API-breaking change.
   380→
   381→## Migration Notes
   382→
   383→### Dropped Props
   384→
   385→| Prop | Reason |
   386→|------|--------|
   387→| `sx` | MUI styling system — never migrated per project policy |
   388→| `reserveArrowSpace` | Not documented in Storybook; dead prop in v0 (defined but never used in implementation) |
   389→| `onPreviousClick` | Not documented in Storybook argTypes |
   390→| `onNextClick` | Not documented in Storybook argTypes |
   391→| `onSlideClick` | Not documented in Storybook argTypes; superseded by `onClick` HTML attribute on root |
   392→
   393→### Changed Internal Dependencies
   394→
   395→| v0 | v1 |
   396→|----|-----|
   397→| `MuiIconButton` from `@mui/material` | `<button>` element |
   398→| `Skeleton` from `@mui/material` | CSS placeholder `<div>` |
   399→| `styled()` from MUI emotion | CSS Modules (`*.module.css`) |
   400→| `Box` from v0 Layout | `<div>` |
   401→| `designTokens` from v2 path | v3 CSS variables |
   402→| v2 `@customink/design-tokens/v2/...` | v3 CSS vars via `@customink/design-tokens/css` |
   403→
   404→### Breaking Changes
   405→
   406→None. All Storybook-documented props preserved.
   407→
   408→## Unspecified Behavior
   409→
   410→| Item | Resolution |
   411→|------|------------|
   412→| Color token mapping | All colors use raw CSS hex/rgba values from v0. v3 semantic token mapping deferred to a later ticket. |
   413→| Arrow/dot transitions | No transitions (none in v0; decided not to add). |
   414→
   415→## Visual Regression Placement
   416→
   417→- **demo app:** Catalog (demo-next) — Carousel is used in product detail image galleries.
   418→- **demo-next Showcase:** Yes — cover the following variants:
   419→  1. Default: arrows + dots, no thumbnails
   420→  2. Thumbnails left (`navigatorSide='left'`)
   421→  3. Thumbnails bottom (`navigatorSide='bottom'`)
   422→  4. Arrow outside (`arrowPosition='outside'`, `arrowOffset=40`)
   423→  5. Gray background (`sliderBg='gray'`)
   424→  6. Multiple slides (`slidesPerView=2`, `slidesSpacing=8`)
   425→  7. No loop (`loop=false`) — verify arrows disable at edges
   426→  8. Small thumbnails (`thumbSize='small'`)
   427→

content.text: Let me check the CardCarousel component to understand if it's a wrapper around the base Carousel:

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a summary document with the findings. ## Summary: v1 Carousel Component Analysis I've located and analyzed the v1 Carousel component in the Pigment Design System. Here are the key findings for your trending products migration: ### File Paths **Main Component:** - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/Carousel.tsx` - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/Carousel.module.css` - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/index.ts` **Specification & Tests:** - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/Carousel.spec.md` - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/Carousel/Carousel.test.tsx` **Related Component (Card-based carousel):** - `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/pigment/packages/react/src/components/CardCarousel/CardCarousel.tsx` ### 1. Full Props API ```typescript type CarouselProps = { arrows?: boolean; // Show prev/next arrows (default: true) arrowPosition?: 'inside' | 'outside'; // Arrow placement (default: 'inside') arrowOffset?: string | number; // Horizontal offset when outside (default: 40) dots?: boolean; // Show dot navigation (default: true) lazy?: boolean; // Enable lazy loading (default: false) lazyOffset?: number; // Adjacent slides to preload (default: 1) loop?: boolean; // Wrap from last to first slide (default: true) navigatorSide?: 'left' | 'bottom'; // Thumbnail position (default: 'left') sliderBg?: 'white' | 'gray' | 'transparent'; // Background (default: 'white') sliderHeight?: string; // Height (default: '350px') ⚠️ FIXED HEIGHT sliderStyle?: React.CSSProperties; // Inline styles for slider sliderOrigin?: 'auto' | 'center'; // Slide origin point slides: React.ReactElement[]; // REQUIRED: array of slide elements slidesPerView?: number; // Visible slides (default: 1) slidesSpacing?: number; // Gap between slides in px (default: 0) thumbnails?: React.ReactElement[]; // Optional thumbnail navigator thumbSize?: 'small' | 'medium'; // Thumbnail size (default: 'medium') onCreated?: (slider: KeenSliderInstance) => void; // Programmatic control } & React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<'div'>; ``` ### 2. Fixed Height Constraint ⚠️ **YES - The Carousel enforces a FIXED height:** ```typescript sliderHeight?: string; // @default '350px' ``` **In the render layer (line 248):** ```typescript
``` **In CSS (Carousel.module.css, line 17-20):** ```css .slider { position: relative; overflow: visible; } ``` The slider container receives an explicit `height: sliderHeight` (defaults to `'350px'`). All slides are rendered with: ```css .slide > * { height: 100%; /* Force slide content to fill the slider height */ } ``` This means all slide content is constrained to the full slider height. **This is unsuitable for variable-height product cards** because: - All cards are forced to `height: 100%` of the slider - The slider has a fixed height (350px by default) - There's no mechanism to dynamically adjust based on card content ### 3. How Slides Are Passed **Via the `slides` prop (required array):** ```typescript slides: React.ReactElement[]; // Required ``` **Usage:** ```typescript , , , ]} /> ``` This is **NOT** `children`-based. Slides must be passed as an explicit prop array. **Optional thumbnail navigation** (separate prop): ```typescript thumbnails?: React.ReactElement[]; // Parallel array of thumbnail elements ``` ### 4. Navigation Controls **The Carousel HANDLES navigation internally** (not exposed directly): - **Prev/Next Arrows:** Built-in buttons; controlled via `arrows`, `arrowPosition`, `arrowOffset` props - **Dot Navigation:** Built-in dots; shown when `dots=true` and no thumbnails - **Thumbnail Navigator:** Built-in thumbnail buttons; shown when `thumbnails` array is provided **Programmatic Control Available via `onCreated` callback:** ```typescript onCreated?: (slider: KeenSliderInstance) => void; ``` Provides access to the `KeenSliderInstance` for methods like: - `slider.moveToIdx(n)` — Jump to slide N - `slider.prev()` — Go to previous slide - `slider.next()` — Go to next slide **Example:** ```typescript const [slider, setSlider] = useState(null); setSlider(instance)} /> ``` ### 5. Suitability for Variable-Height Product Cards **NOT SUITABLE** — Multiple constraints: 1. **Fixed slider height:** Default 350px, hardcoded in render. Even if you override with `sliderHeight`, all cards are forced to that height via CSS. 2. **CSS height constraint:** `.slide > *` has `height: 100%`, forcing all child content to fill the slider height. 3. **No responsive height:** The component doesn't measure or adapt to card heights. The keen-slider is configured with a fixed viewport height. 4. **Not designed for cards with varying content:** Built for image galleries where height uniformity is expected. **Alternative: `CardCarousel`** For variable-height card layouts, the codebase includes `/packages/react/src/components/CardCarousel/CardCarousel.tsx` which: - Accepts children (not a `slides` array) - Uses `perView: 3` slides with responsive breakpoints (not a single fixed height) - Handles variable-height cards naturally (cards are flex items, not stretched) - Still uses keen-slider but with different configuration **CardCarousel props:** ```typescript type CardCarouselProps = { loop?: boolean; // Default: true autoPlay?: boolean; // Default: false children: React.ReactNode; // Children-based, not array } & Omit, 'children'>; ``` --- **Recommendation:** For trending products with variable-height cards, investigate **CardCarousel** instead of the base Carousel, or consider custom height management outside the component.
